videos.title: Paper Folding and Symmetry | Completing Figures
videos.description: Learn to fold paper to find symmetry and complete symmetrical figures.
learningObjectives: Fold paper and cut shapes to create symmetrical figures
Predict what a folded-and-cut shape will look like when opened
keyConceptsCovered: Folding paper along the line of symmetry
Cutting folded paper creates symmetrical shapes
Completing the other half of a figure

videos.title: Symmetrical Shapes | How Many Lines of Symmetry?
videos.description: Find lines of symmetry in different shapes like squares, rectangles, circles, and triangles.
learningObjectives: Use knowledge of symmetrical shapes to fold and cut correctly
Predict outcomes of folding and cutting regular shapes
Apply lines of symmetry to complete figures
keyConceptsCovered: Using shape symmetry to plan folds and cuts
Completing figures based on shape properties

videos.title: Mirror Images and Reflections | Geometry
videos.description: Understand how mirror images work and how to draw reflections of shapes.
learningObjectives: Use mirror image concept to complete figures
Draw the missing half of a symmetrical figure
keyConceptsCovered: The missing half is the mirror image of the given half
Using the line of symmetry as the mirror line
Checking completed figures with a mirror

videos.title: Equal Halves and Parts | Folding Shapes in Half
videos.description: Learn what it means to divide a shape into two equal halves. When both halves match perfectly, that is symmetry!
learningObjectives: Practise folding to verify completed figures
Use equal halves concept in paper cutting activities
Build confidence with hands-on symmetry tasks
keyConceptsCovered: Folding verifies if the figure is completed correctly
Equal halves from folding and cutting

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Core folding and completing concepts for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of folding, cutting, and completing with mirror image support

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Halves review first, then all folding and completing content

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites
after_prerequisite_review.rationale: Essential folding and completing concepts only

questions.question: Sita folds a piece of paper in half and cuts out a half-heart shape along the fold. When she opens the paper, what will she see?
questions.explanation: When you fold paper, cut a half-shape along the fold, and open it, you get the full symmetrical shape. A half-heart becomes a full heart!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: This is a hands-on topic! Have students fold paper and cut shapes. Ask them to predict what the opened shape will look like before they unfold. Give grid paper for completing half-drawn figures.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Do paper folding and cutting activities with your child. Fold paper in half, cut out shapes, and open to see the symmetrical result. Try drawing half a butterfly and asking your child to complete the other half.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: This is the most fun part! Fold paper, cut shapes, and open them up to see beautiful symmetrical designs. Try making snowflakes by folding paper into quarters and cutting!
